questions.question: How much of the year does the prohibition of gas leaf blowers last? What date in the year does it start, and what date does it end?
questions.answer: The prohibition of gas leaf blowers lasts for 6 months of the year, starting on December 15 and ending on March 15, and again from May 15 to October 15.

questions.question: What are the permitted hours for gas leaf blower operation?
questions.answer: Gas leaf blowers may only be operated from 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. on Monday through Saturday, excluding holidays.

questions.question: What are the penalties for violating gas leaf blower regulations?
questions.answer: The penalties for violating gas leaf blower regulations are as follows: a fine up to $250 for the first offense, up to $500 for the second offense, and up to $1,000 for the third offense and any subsequent offenses.

questions.question: Are there any exceptions for school/government/commercial properties?
questions.answer: There are no exceptions stated for school, government, or commercial properties regarding the gas leaf blower regulations.

questions.question: How are violations reported and enforced?
questions.answer: Violations of gas leaf blower regulations can be reported and enforced by any parking enforcement officer or a member of the Village of Bronxville Police Department, who is authorized to issue and serve an appearance ticket.

questions.question: Does the ordinance mention the benefits of leaving leaf debris and/or leaf mulching?
questions.answer: The ordinance does not mention the benefits of leaving leaf debris or leaf mulching.

questions.question: Does the ordinance require a report to be presented to the public?
questions.answer: The ordinance does not require a report to be presented to the public.
